session_summary: Major session — built sim data-collection pipeline equivalent to real-world. Drop_sweep self-contained, calibrated home pose (QS + Record Sim only), cup padding 0%, sim_reset uses new match_real_world MCP tool, Record Sim 1:1 episode contract w/ xdotool key signaling to lerobot, action-mirror swapped from /joint_states to controller_state.reference (BC-portability fix). External parity audit received as ~/transfer/SIM_DATASET_PARITY_REPORT.md — identifies 6 must-fix items remaining for full real-equivalence.

completed_tasks.name: drop_sweep self-contained (combined pan+tilt, no drop_point dependency)
expose match_real_world MCP tool in soarm101-dt
sim_reset.sh uses match_real_world+update_cups for quick_start-equivalent cup poses
_cmd_grasp_home_data calibrated to real-world ep7 frame 277 shoulders+gripper, sim wrist
Record Sim — 1:1 episode contract via xdotool Right/Left to lerobot, phase-A gate, drain window
joint_states_to_commands.py rewritten — reads controller_state.reference, not /joint_states (action≠state per frame)
CLAUDE.md updates — use lerobot-dataset-viz, never custom rerun loader (ffmpeg 4.4 vs rerun ≥5.1)
Recorded sim_blue_v6_action_test_175034 (3 episodes, 10/10 success in v5 had been 10) and copied to ~/transfer/

remaining_tasks.name: Audit item 1: change task prompt from 'sort blue blocks' to 'Pick a blue lego and place it in blue cup'
Audit item 2: tasks.parquet.task dtype 'string' → 'large_string'
remaining_tasks.effort: investigate lerobot version skew or PyArrow cast

remaining_tasks.name: Audit item 4: joint range / units / calibration alignment (wrist_roll −38° offset, gripper 5× narrower range — units mismatch)
remaining_tasks.effort: deep URDF work — gripper joint angle vs jaw-opening units, wrist_roll zero pose to match Feetech

remaining_tasks.name: Audit item 6: episode length 459 frames → ~278 (real)
remaining_tasks.effort: split _rec_run_one_qs_cycle into prep+execute, run prep during Phase B (Tier 2 refactor)

remaining_tasks.name: Try headless Isaac Sim to lift camera FPS (workspace 13 Hz, wrist 22 Hz vs target 30 Hz)
remaining_tasks.effort: --/app/window/enabled=false on Kit launch + verify cameras/MCP socket still come up

blockers.description: Gripper joint units mismatch — sim publishes ~6.5° max, real shows 29.4°. Need to confirm whether sim publishes joint angle (moving jaw rotation) vs jaw-opening angle, then align with Feetech's reported convention. URDF inspection needed.

wrist_roll zero pose — sim convention has wrist_roll near -π/2 at home; real has -49° at home. Either sim URDF needs zero-pose pin to match Feetech, or BC training has to learn the offset (unsupported).
blockers.workaround: Sim convention currently used by drop_sweep IK (lock_pan to -π/2 in IK). Changing it requires updating drop_sweep + grasp_move + grasp_home_data simultaneously.

human_actions_pending.action: Decide on the exact task prompt string
human_actions_pending.context: User may want to use different prompts per dataset for training diversity. Default to real-canonical 'Pick a blue lego and place it in blue cup' for parity.

human_actions_pending.action: Review whether 40 or 440 episodes is the target for next batch
human_actions_pending.context: 440 = full real-side parity. 40 = absolute floor for 25/75 ratio sweeps. Records take ~25s each so 440 = ~3 hours unattended.

decisions.decision: drop_sweep is self-contained (no drop_point pre-step) and uses cup-derived pan locked
decisions.rationale: Combined pan+tilt motion clears cups in tier-1 deterministic path; eliminates need for separate drop_point button auto-call
2.5% caused drop_sweep carried-block clip at sub-mm during pan-across, falling through to OMPL RNG fallback. 0% gives tier-1 deterministic clearance for both grasp_move + drop_sweep.

decisions.decision: Calibrated home pose for QS + Record Sim ONLY (manual button, IK tab, Real Test tab keep original all-zeros + sim wrist)
decisions.rationale: Sim recordings should start from the same pose as real captures; other paths use the canonical zero-shoulders home for IK reference.

decisions.decision: 1:1 episode contract via xdotool key injection to lerobot's pynput global listener
decisions.rationale: lerobot's keyboard listener catches Right/Left arrow regardless of focus on X11. Wayland-incompatible by design but matches current session model.

Real-world teleop has action leading state by ~tracking lag; mirroring /joint_states made action == state to 14 decimals which is uninformative for BC. Now per-frame action ≠ state at sub-degree level.

decisions.decision: Use lerobot-dataset-viz canonically; never write custom rerun loaders
decisions.rationale: Custom AssetVideo path failed on Ubuntu 22.04 (ffmpeg 4.4 < rerun's 5.1). Official tool decodes via pyav inside pixi-Jazzy and bypasses the issue. Documented in linux-env/CLAUDE.md gotchas.

infrastructure_state.joint_states_to_commands_mirror: alive at PID ~3566950 — running NEW controller-reference version, publishing /joint_commands_lerobot at 30 Hz
infrastructure_state.rerun_viewer: may be alive at :9876 (last spawned by lerobot-dataset-viz on ep0 of v6); state varies
datasets: ~/.cache/huggingface/lerobot/local/sim_blue_v5_171525/ (10 episodes, action mirror OLD version — keep for diff reference)

next_action: Read ~/transfer/SIM_DATASET_PARITY_REPORT.md fully. Then fix audit item 1 (task prompt) as warm-up, then tackle item 4 (gripper units) by inspecting the sim URDF gripper joint definition and comparing with Feetech's degree convention. Item 5 (record 40+ episodes) and item 6 (episode length trim via Phase-B prep) are downstream of fixing 1+4. Verify after each fix using the 5-step recipe in the parity report.
context_notes: Session was very productive — went from broken 1-cycle pick-place to a working sim recording pipeline that's lerobot-tool-compatible (lerobot-dataset-viz opens it identically to real datasets). User explicitly requested fixing parity in the next session ('the first thing we do is fix the parity and verify they are the exact same as the real world dataset'). The audit report is the canonical punch list. 6 of 6 audit items are well-understood — 3 are 1-line fixes, 1 is a deep URDF/calibration story, 2 are medium refactors. Don't pre-optimize headless or other Tier 2 items until parity is closed.
